title: Classes & Objects 🏡
description: Learn the difference between a blueprint and a real house.

slides.title: Representing the Real World 🌍
slides.content:
# The Limits of Variables

If you want to make a game with a Player, how do you store their data?
`String playerName = 'Mario';`
`int playerHealth = 100;`
`int playerLives = 3;`

This is messy! A Player is a single concept, but your data is scattered across three different variables.
**Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)** lets us bundle all this related data into a single, unified "Object".

slides.title: Classes vs Objects 🏗️
slides.content:
## Blueprints and Houses

Before you build a house, the architect draws a **Blueprint**. You can't live in the blueprint, but it perfectly describes the structure.

Then, the construction workers use that ONE blueprint to build five REAL **Houses** in a neighborhood.

In Dart:
* A **Class** is the blueprint.
* An **Object** is the real, physical house built from that blueprint!

quizData.question: What is the relationship between a Class and an Object?

options.text: They are exactly the same thing.
A Class is the blueprint/template, and an Object is the physical item created from it.
An Object is the blueprint, and the Class is the item.

quizData.explanation: You write a Class once. You use it to spawn as many Objects as you want.

slides.title: Writing a Class 📝

quizData.explanation: The 'class' keyword initiates the blueprint structure.
